search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae hypoglycaemia diabetes telehealth guideline UAE PDF
site:diabetesjournals.org Standards of Care 2026 hypoglycemia recommendations awareness severe event
site:diabetesjournals.org 2026 hypoglycemia risk assessment driving glucagon education
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Dose and correction instructions were prohibited.
Non-official summaries were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Partial: ADA recommendations cover people with diabetes broadly, while the DHA source is adult type 2 diabetes and the legacy workflow has no diabetes-type or age restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for event review and clinician-led prevention documentation, not emergency treatment, medicine dosing, or automatic management.
UAE_applicability: The DHA adult telehealth diabetes source provides local glucose-awareness, home-monitoring, HbA1c, and profound-hypoglycaemia context; ADA details require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The official DHA issue 2 PDF and official 2026 ADA Standards section were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: DHA issue 2 remains within its stated revision cycle; ADA 2026 is current for the active research date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult type 2 diabetes home-glucose, HbA1c, and hypoglycaemia-awareness review fields with scope qualifiers retained.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed for profound hypoglycaemia context; no emergency conclusion or automatic referral item is mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-led treatment-plan review, follow-up, education, and glucagon-discussion documentation without treatment instructions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports reviewing hypoglycaemia history at encounters, awareness, high-risk or severe events, clinician-led treatment-plan reevaluation, structured education, and glucagon discussion without inserting treatment instructions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped event identity, glucose-value, severe-assistance, loss-of-consciousness, and seizure documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines clinically relevant glucose and severe-event context, including events requiring assistance and possible loss of consciousness or seizure, without diagnosing an individual event.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped episode frequency, timing, symptoms, medication, meal or activity, awareness, glucose, and history-draft documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting event frequency, severity, precipitants, symptoms, treatment approach, glucose readings, medication context, awareness, and risk factors.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped driving-safety and monitoring context without generating advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports meal, activity, illness, monitoring, driving-safety, education, and glucagon context as clinician-reviewed documentation only.

unresolved_source_gaps: The legacy workflow does not encode diabetes type, age, pregnancy, medicine class, or all high-risk qualifiers.
Alcohol context, broad vitals, general appearance, weight, blood pressure, generic clinician plan, safety-net wording, fixed follow-up intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, emergency response, glucose correction, treatment, medicine selection or dose, driving restriction, and referral remain clinician responsibilities.
